title: Augwynne Djo
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Dathomiri |image=[[File:Augwynne_Djo.jpg]] |name=Augwynne Djo |homeworld=[[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]]''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[The Last Jedi (novel)|''The Last Jedi'']] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair=White |feathers= |eyes=Pale |skin=Pale |cyber= |families=[[Singing Mountain Clan]] |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=*[[Allaya Djo]] *[[Barukka]]''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' *[[Gethzerion]] *[[Kara'Teel]] *[[Aelta]] {{C|adopted}}{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=general|Gethzerion|Dathomir}} *[[Chastina]] {{C|adopted}} |affiliation= *[[Djo family]] *[[New Republic]] *[[Witches of Dathomir]] **Singing Mountain Clan |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Never concede to evil. That is our oldest and most sacred law. When we concede to evil, even in a small way, we feed it, and it grows stronger.|Augwynne Djo|}} '''Augwynne Djo''' was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who was the [[Clan mother/Legends|clan mother]] of the [[Singing Mountain Clan]] of the [[Witches of Dathomir]], and eventually rose to be the nominal [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] of [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] by [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]; however, this was little more than a [[Monarchy/Legends|constitutional formality]]. Djo was the grandmother of [[Teneniel Djo]], great-grandmother of [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka]], and great-great grandmother of [[Allana Djo Solo|Allana Solo]]. Augywnne Djo had many adventures as clan mother. She once helped [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jax Pavan]] on his journey to learn the secrets of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Ramage]]. She also once drove away the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]], another witch clan, with help from the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and gained the deed to Dathomir in exchange for 3 favors requested by [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== {{Quote|You are Augwynne Djo, are you not?|Jax Pavan|}} [[Reproduction/Legends|Born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] decades before the [[Declaration of a New Order|founding]] of [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Augwynne Djo was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] and a member of the [[Witches of Dathomir]], a society dominated by [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] women. ===Clan mother=== {{Quote|The Singing Mountain clan is in constant conflict with the Nightsisters, another clan of Force-users here on Dathomir.|Augwynne Djo|{{GalaxiesAED}} and''[[The Nightsister's Revenge]]'' both take place during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].}} At some point before the rise of the Empire in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']] to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. she had become the [[Clan mother/Legends|clan mother]] of the [[Singing Mountain Clan]], a [[Coven/Legends|coven]] of witches that claimed the territory located in the vicinity of an [[Singing Mountain (Dathomir)/Legends|eponymous]] landform. Mother Augwynne and her [[Sister (Witches of Dathomir)|sisters]] were the guardians of a ruined [[Star Temple]], a type of mysterious pyramid built by the ancient, [[Extragalactic/Legends|extragalactic]]{{FactFile|139|DAT 1-4|Dathomir}} [[Kwa/Legends|Kwa]] [[Species/Legends|species]] in order to protect a [[Infinity Gate|powerful artifact]]''[[Star Wars: Infinity's End]]'' capable of transport across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. ====Familial troubles==== During her tenure as clan mother, the life of Augwynne Djo unfolded in tragedy. All four of her daughters ended up being lost to her, either through [[Death/Legends|death]] or betrayal. Djo's oldest daughter, [[Barukka]], had dabbled in the forbidden teachings of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], and the clan mother had been forced to cast her out. Another one of her daughters, [[Allaya Djo]], acted as the clan's warmaster and bore a daughter named [[Teneniel Djo|Teneniel]], but met an untimely death in battle. But worst of all was the loss of [[Gethzerion]], who had always been Mother Augwynne's favorite. Gethzerion and Barukka, both lost to the dark side, became [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]]. Gethzerion and her friend [[Baritha]] even attempted to murder their own mother, a plan that was only foiled by the sacrifice of their youngest sister [[Kara'Teel]], who gave her life to protect Augwynne. However, following these tragedies, Augwynne decided to adopt [[Aelta]]. ====Star Temples==== In 19 BBY,[[The Last Jedi (novel)|''The Last Jedi'']] dates itself to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 years before the]] events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of the film to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], thus the book must take place in 19 BBY. [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jax Pavan]] visited Dathomir in order to search a Star Temple. After landing on the Singing Mounain, Jax was escorted to Djo's quarters by sisters [[Magash Drashi]] and [[Duala Aidu]]. After conversing for a bit, Djo granted him permission to journey to the Star Temple. When Pavan returned from the temple, Djo and the other sisters agreed to help Pavan open the [[SD41263.I]] [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] in order to learn the secrets of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Ramage]]. The holocron would only activate after being provided [[Blood/Legends|blood]] from a Force-sensitive being. Djo, Aida, Pavan, and Drashi each donated blood to open the holocron. When the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] returned from his [[Infiltration of Kantaros Station|infiltration]] of [[Kantaros Station]], Pavan had his mind cleared of his dark knowledge by Djo, per a request Pavan had made prior. ====Era of Rebellion==== During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Augwynne informed Jedi Knight [[Rachi Sitra]] about a danger one of the Nightsisters, [[Kyrisa]], had represented to her clan.{{SWGTCG|set=The Nightsister's Revenge|scenario=Light Side scenario campaign}} After the murder of her daughter Aelta at the hands of Gethzerion, Augwynne adopted [[Chastina]] and gave her a [[Necklace/Legends|necklace]] once belonging to Gethzerion. [[File:Augwynnedjo.jpg|left|thumb|Augwynne Djo during the era of the New Republic.]] In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'' to [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]. Augwynne was present when [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] members [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] arrived at the Singing Mountain Clan. Djo declared Solo a free man from the [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] of the Nightsisters after Organa presented his case. Learning Solo owned the deed to Dathomir, she gave him the chance to make three requests in order to gain it. Solo asked Djo to get him off Dathomir, give him the [[Gold/Legends|gold]] and gems that were plentiful on the planet, and find a way to get Organa to marry him. However, she did not complete Solo's third request, as she believed she could not force Organa to marry him. Augwynne went to her chambers, conversing with the [[Council of Sisters]] ([[Tannath]], [[Azbeth]], [[Shen (Dathomiri)|Shen]]) about the danger of fighting the Nightsisters. Djo had no choice but to lead the sisters of the Singing Mountain Clan against the Nightsisters in order to give Solo time to get the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] off Dathomir. She requested help from the [[Red Hills Clan]] and [[Frenzied River Clan]] however neither clan offered forces or assistance. Thanks to Jedi Knight [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Chume'da]] [[Isolder]] of the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]], Solo, Organa, Chewbacca, and her granddaughter, Teneniel, the sisters of the Singing Mountain Clan were able to drive the Nightsisters away. Augwynne and the sisters were finally rid of the Nightsisters when they were shot down trying to escape Dathomir by the forces of [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Zsinj]] whose [[Zsinj's Empire|empire]] ruled the [[Quelii sector/Legends|Quelii sector]]. After the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Dathomir]], Augwynne became the owner of Dathomir when Solo turned over the deed. Around the same time, her surviving daughter, Barukka, returned from the dark and rejoined the clan, much to Augwynne's joy. Augwynne advocated for Dathomir's membership in the [[New Republic]]. ====Later life==== {{Quote|Does Augwynne Djo still rule here?"
"Yes. My great-grandmother.|Luke Skywalker and Tenel Ka|''[[Young Jedi Knights: Shadow Academy]]''}} In [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Young Jedi Knights: Shadow Academy]]'' to [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]]. Skywalker and Augwynne's great-granddaughter, [[Tenel Ka Djo]], came to Dathomir for Augwynne's help in finding the resurgent Nightsisters and the [[Shadow Academy]]. Augwynne spoke with the Council of Sisters. When returning, she stated to Skywalker that visitors would be coming by and could answer their questions. After [[Great Canyon Clan]] visitors [[Vonnda Ra]] and [[Vilas]] came by and told the story of the Nightsisters, Skywalker and Tenel decided to go to the [[Great Canyon (Dathomir)/Legends|Great Canyon]]. Before their departure, Augwynne gave them cloaks and hugged Tenel, embracing her before sending them off. That same [[Standard year/Legends|year]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Young Jedi Knights: Lightsabers]]'' to [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]]. Augwynne's favorite [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]] died and Tenel Ka kept two of the animal's smaller teeth, which she used as the hilt for [[Tenel Ka Djo's lightsaber|her]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].''[[Young Jedi Knights: Lightsabers]]'' In [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Young Jedi Knights: Crisis at Crystal Reef]]'' to [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]]. she and the rest of the [[Djo family]] visited Tenel when she graduated from the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].''[[Young Jedi Knights: Crisis at Crystal Reef]]'' ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|But, Mother, he is a stranger. And a man."
"He is a Jedi.|Magash Drashi and Augwynne Djo|}} Djo had pale [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and [[Hair/Legends|hair]] the [[Color/Legends|color]] of moonlight. She was tall and regal, with facial features considered handsome by her fellow Humans, as well as graceful, long-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]]. By 19 BBY, she had the appearance of a sixty-year old woman though she was many centuries older. Unlike her fellow sisters, Djo was more welcome to outsiders. She was shrewd and calculating, having been able to manipulate Han Solo into the bargain that gained her the deed to Dathomir. She tended to stick to her clan laws, but tended to break them at times. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|If I survive my … attempt to rescue my friend, I wish to return here and have you purge this knowledge from my mind. Utterly purge it, so that it will never be misused, either by Vader or by me."
"As you wish.|Jax Pavan and Augwynne Djo|}} Djo was skilled in [[Dathomir Magic|Allyan Magic]], exhibiting many [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]]. Using the Force, she could [[Tutaminis|absorb]] and dissipate energy, [[Force healing/Legends|heal]], [[Control Pain|control pain]], conjure [[Force barrier/Legends|shields]], [[Projective Telepathy|communicate with others at long distances]], [[Receptive Telepathy|read people's thoughts and feel their emotions]], use [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]], [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Kill|kill]] people telekinetically, [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Whirlwind|create storms telekinetically]], [[Comprehend Speech|comprehend speech]], [[Transfer Force|transfer force]], and [[Drain Knowledge|drain people of knowledge]]. She also knew how to swim, which she taught her great-granddaughter, Tenel Ka. ==Equipment== When in full regalia, Mother Djo wore a tunic of glittering yellow scales over a hide robe with black lizard shapes sewn around the hem. Her ceremonial headdress was made of smooth golden [[Wood/Legends|wood]], and was decorated with cabochons of yellow tigereye. On other occasions, Djo was known to wear a coronet of [[Aurodium/Legends|aurodium]]. ==Behind the scenes== Augwynne Djo first appeared in the [[1994]] novel, ''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'', written by [[Dave Wolverton]]. Oddly, ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' states that she was the one to have married Han and Leia Solo, however this contradicts ''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]''. This article assumes the ''The Courtship of Princess Leia'' is correct. ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' incorrectly states that Augwynne took over as the clan's mother due to the death of her daughter Allaya.''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' However, multiple sources establish that she was mother long before the death of Allaya.{{GalaxiesAED}} This article assumes that those sources are correct. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Augwynne Djo}} *[[The Last Jedi (novel)|''The Last Jedi'']] *{{SWGTCG|set=The Nightsister's Revenge|scenario=Light Side scenario campaign}} *{{GalaxiesAED}} {{Mo}} *''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'' {{Ab|The Courtship of Princess Leia (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[The Courtship of Princess Leia (abridged audiobook)|''The Courtship of Princess Leia'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Young Jedi Knights: Shadow Academy]]'' *''[[Young Jedi Knights: Lightsabers]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Young Jedi Knights: Crisis at Crystal Reef]]'' ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *{{FactFile|76|SOL 21-22|Han Solo}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{SWGTCG|set=The Nightsister's Revenge|cardname=Augwynne Djo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Augwynne Djo }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Djo, Augwynne}} [[Category:Clan mothers]] [[Category:Djo family]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Singing Mountain Clan members]]
